tools.name: calls_payments_update
tools.description: update an instance of payments with different phases of payment flows. Required: AccountSid, CallSid, Sid, IdempotencyKey, StatusCallback. Optional: Capture, Status. Returns the new ApiV2010AccountCallPayments.

params.name: AccountSid
params.description: The SID of the [Account](https://www.twilio.com/docs/iam/api/account) that will update the resource.

params.name: CallSid
params.description: The SID of the call that will update the resource. This should be the same call sid that was used to create payments resource.

params.name: Sid
params.description: The SID of Payments session that needs to be updated.

params.name: Capture
params.description: The piece of payment information that you wish the caller to enter. Must be one of `payment-card-number`, `expiration-date`, `security-code`, `postal-code`, `bank-routing-number`, `bank-account-number`, or their `-matcher` variants for input confirmation when `RequireMatchingInputs` is enabled.

params.name: IdempotencyKey
params.description: A unique token that will be used to ensure that multiple API calls with the same information do not result in multiple transactions. This should be a unique string value per API call and can be a randomly generated.

params.name: Status
params.description: Indicates whether the current payment session should be cancelled or completed. When `cancel` the payment session is cancelled. When `complete`, Twilio sends the payment information to the selected Pay Connector for processing.

params.name: StatusCallback
params.description: Provide an absolute or relative URL to receive status updates regarding your Pay session. Read more about the [Update](https://www.twilio.com/docs/voice/api/payment-resource#statuscallback-update) and [Complete/Cancel](https://www.twilio.com/docs/voice/api/payment-resource#statuscallback-cancelcomplete) POST requests.

tools.name: calls_payments-json_create-payments
tools.description: create an instance of payments. This will start a new payments session. Required: AccountSid, CallSid, IdempotencyKey, StatusCallback. Optional: BankAccountType, ChargeAmount, Confirmation (plus 13 more). Returns the new ApiV2010AccountCallPayments.

params.name: AccountSid
params.description: The SID of the [Account](https://www.

params.name: CallSid
params.description: The SID of the call that will create the resource. Call leg associated with this sid is expected to provide payment information thru DTMF.

params.name: BankAccountType
params.description: Type of bank account if payment source is ACH. One of `consumer-checking`, `consumer-savings`, or `commercial-checking`. The default value is `consumer-checking`.

params.name: ChargeAmount
params.description: A positive decimal value less than 1,000,000 to charge against the credit card or bank account. Default currency can be overwritten with `currency` field. Leave blank or set to 0 to tokenize.

params.name: Confirmation
params.description: Whether to prompt the caller to confirm their payment information before submitting to the payment gateway. If `true`, the caller will hear the last 4 digits of their card or account number and must press 1 to confirm or 2 to cancel. Default is `false`.

params.name: Currency
params.description: The currency of the `charge_amount`, formatted as [ISO 4127](http://www.iso.org/iso/home/standards/currency_codes.htm) format. The default value is `USD` and all values allowed from the Pay Connector are accepted.

params.name: Description
params.description: The description can be used to provide more details regarding the transaction. This information is submitted along with the payment details to the Payment Connector which are then posted on the transactions.

params.name: IdempotencyKey
params.description: A unique token that will be used to ensure that multiple API calls with the same information do not result in multiple transactions. This should be a unique string value per API call and can be a randomly generated.

params.name: Input
params.description: A list of inputs that should be accepted. Currently only `dtmf` is supported. All digits captured during a pay session are redacted from the logs.

params.name: MinPostalCodeLength
params.description: A positive integer that is used to validate the length of the `PostalCode` inputted by the user. User must enter this many digits.

params.name: Parameter
params.description: A single-level JSON object used to pass custom parameters to payment processors. (Required for ACH payments). The information that has to be included here depends on the <Pay> Connector. [Read more](https://www.twilio.com/console/voice/pay-connectors).

params.name: PaymentConnector
params.description: This is the unique name corresponding to the Pay Connector installed in the Twilio Add-ons. Learn more about [<Pay> Connectors](https://www.twilio.com/console/voice/pay-connectors). The default value is `Default`.

params.name: PaymentMethod
params.description: Type of payment being captured. One of `credit-card` or `ach-debit`. The default value is `credit-card`.

params.name: PostalCode
params.description: Indicates whether the credit card postal code (zip code) is a required piece of payment information that must be provided by the caller. The default is `true`.

params.name: RequireMatchingInputs
params.description: A comma-separated list of payment information fields that require the caller to enter the same value twice for confirmation. Supported values are `payment-card-number`, `expiration-date`, `security-code`, and `postal-code`.

params.name: SecurityCode
params.description: Indicates whether the credit card security code is a required piece of payment information that must be provided by the caller. The default is `true`.

params.name: StatusCallback
params.description: Provide an absolute or relative URL to receive status updates regarding your Pay session. Read more about the [expected StatusCallback values](https://www.twilio.com/docs/voice/api/payment-resource#statuscallback)

params.name: Timeout
params.description: The number of seconds that <Pay> should wait for the caller to press a digit between each subsequent digit, after the first one, before moving on to validate the digits captured. The default is `5`, maximum is `600`.

params.name: TokenType
params.description: Indicates whether the payment method should be tokenized as a `one-time`, `reusable`, or `payment-method` token. The default value is `reusable`. Do not enter a charge amount when tokenizing. If a charge amount is entered, the payment method will be charged and not tokenized.

params.name: ValidCardTypes
params.description: Credit card types separated by space that Pay should accept. The default value is `visa mastercard amex`

params.name: CallSid

params.description: The number of channels used in the recording. Can be: `mono` or `dual` and the default is `mono`. `mono` records all parties of the call into one channel. `dual` records each party of a 2-party call into separate channels.

params.name: RecordingConfigurationId
params.description: The identifier of the configuration to be used when creating and processing the recording

params.name: RecordingStatusCallback
params.description: The URL we should call using the `recording_status_callback_method` on each recording event specified in `recording_status_callback_event`. For more information, see [RecordingStatusCallback parameters](https://www.twilio.com/docs/voice/api/recording#recordingstatuscallback).

params.name: RecordingStatusCallbackEvent
params.description: The recording status events on which we should call the `recording_status_callback` URL. Can be: `in-progress`, `completed` and `absent` and the default is `completed`. Separate multiple event values with a space.

params.name: RecordingStatusCallbackMethod
params.description: The HTTP method we should use to call `recording_status_callback`. Can be: `GET` or `POST` and the default is `POST`.

params.name: RecordingTrack
params.description: The audio track to record for the call. Can be: `inbound`, `outbound` or `both`. The default is `both`. `inbound` records the audio that is received by Twilio. `outbound` records the audio that is generated from Twilio. `both` records the audio that is received and generated by Twilio.

params.name: Trim
params.description: Whether to trim any leading and trailing silence in the recording. Can be: `trim-silence` or `do-not-trim` and the default is `do-not-trim`. `trim-silence` trims the silence from the beginning and end of the recording and `do-not-trim` does not.

tools.name: calls-json_create-call
tools.description: Create a new outgoing call to phones, SIP-enabled endpoints or Twilio Client connections. Required: AccountSid, From, To. Optional: ApplicationSid, AsyncAmd, AsyncAmdStatusCallback (plus 32 more). Returns the new ApiV2010AccountCall.

params.name: AccountSid
params.description: The SID of the [Account](https://www.

params.name: ApplicationSid
params.description: The SID of the Application resource that will handle the call, if the call will be handled by an application.

params.name: AsyncAmd
params.description: Select whether to perform answering machine detection in the background. Default, blocks the execution of the call until Answering Machine Detection is completed. Can be: `true` or `false`.

params.name: AsyncAmdStatusCallback
params.description: The URL that we should call using the `async_amd_status_callback_method` to notify customer application whether the call was answered by human, machine or fax.

params.name: AsyncAmdStatusCallbackMethod
params.description: The HTTP method we should use when calling the `async_amd_status_callback` URL. Can be: `GET` or `POST` and the default is `POST`.

params.name: Byoc
params.description: The SID of a BYOC (Bring Your Own Carrier) trunk to route this call with. Note that `byoc` is only meaningful when `to` is a phone number; it will otherwise be ignored. (Beta)

params.name: CallReason
params.description: The Reason for the outgoing call. Use it to specify the purpose of the call that is presented on the called party's phone. (Branded Calls Beta)

params.name: CallToken
params.description: A token string needed to invoke a forwarded call. A call_token is generated when an incoming call is received on a Twilio number. Pass an incoming call's call_token value to a forwarded call via the call_token parameter when creating a new call. A forwarded call should bear the same CallerID of the original incoming call.

params.name: CallerId
params.description: The phone number, SIP address, or Client identifier that made this call. Phone numbers are in [E.164 format](https://wwnw.twilio.com/docs/glossary/what-e164) (e.g., +16175551212). SIP addresses are formatted as `name@company.com`.

params.name: ClientNotificationUrl
params.description: The URL that we should use to deliver `push call notification`.

params.name: FallbackMethod
params.description: The HTTP method that we should use to request the `fallback_url`. Can be: `GET` or `POST` and the default is `POST`. If an `application_sid` parameter is present, this parameter is ignored.

params.name: FallbackUrl
params.description: The URL that we call using the `fallback_method` if an error occurs when requesting or executing the TwiML at `url`. If an `application_sid` parameter is present, this parameter is ignored.

params.name: From
params.description: The phone number or client identifier to use as the caller id. If using a phone number, it must be a Twilio number or a Verified [outgoing caller id](https://www.twilio.com/docs/voice/api/outgoing-caller-ids) for your account. If the `to` parameter is a phone number, `From` must also be a phone number.

params.name: MachineDetection
params.description: Whether to detect if a human, answering machine, or fax has picked up the call. Can be: `Enable` or `DetectMessageEnd`. Use `Enable` if you would like us to return `AnsweredBy` as soon as the called party is identified. Use `DetectMessageEnd`, if you would like to leave a message on an answering machine. If `send_digits` is provided, this parameter is ignored. For more information, see [Answering Machine Detection](https://www.twilio.com/docs/voice/answering-machine-detection).

params.name: MachineDetectionSilenceTimeout
params.description: The number of milliseconds of initial silence after which an `unknown` AnsweredBy result will be returned. Possible Values: 2000-10000. Default: 5000.

params.name: MachineDetectionSpeechEndThreshold
params.description: The number of milliseconds of silence after speech activity at which point the speech activity is considered complete. Possible Values: 500-5000. Default: 1200.

params.name: MachineDetectionSpeechThreshold
params.description: The number of milliseconds that is used as the measuring stick for the length of the speech activity, where durations lower than this value will be interpreted as a human and longer than this value as a machine. Possible Values: 1000-6000. Default: 2400.

params.name: MachineDetectionTimeout
params.description: The number of seconds that we should attempt to detect an answering machine before timing out and sending a voice request with `AnsweredBy` of `unknown`. The default timeout is 30 seconds.

params.name: Method
params.description: The HTTP method we should use when calling the `url` parameter's value. Can be: `GET` or `POST` and the default is `POST`. If an `application_sid` parameter is present, this parameter is ignored.

params.name: Record
params.description: Whether to record the call. Can be `true` to record the phone call, or `false` to not. The default is `false`. The `recording_url` is sent to the `status_callback` URL.

params.name: RecordingChannels
params.description: The number of channels in the final recording. Can be: `mono` or `dual`. The default is `mono`. `mono` records both legs of the call in a single channel of the recording file. `dual` records each leg to a separate channel of the recording file. The first channel of a dual-channel recording contains the parent call and the second channel contains the child call.

params.name: RecordingConfigurationId
params.description: The identifier of the configuration to be used when creating and processing the recording

params.name: RecordingStatusCallback
params.description: The URL that we call when the recording is available to be accessed.

params.name: RecordingStatusCallbackEvent
params.description: The recording status events that will trigger calls to the URL specified in `recording_status_callback`. Can be: `in-progress`, `completed` and `absent`. Defaults to `completed`. Separate multiple values with a space.

params.name: RecordingStatusCallbackMethod
params.description: The HTTP method we should use when calling the `recording_status_callback` URL. Can be: `GET` or `POST` and the default is `POST`.

params.name: RecordingTrack
params.description: The audio track to record for the call. Can be: `inbound`, `outbound` or `both`. The default is `both`. `inbound` records the audio that is received by Twilio. `outbound` records the audio that is generated from Twilio. `both` records the audio that is received and generated by Twilio.

params.name: SendDigits
params.description: The string of keys to dial after connecting to the number, with a maximum length of 32 digits. Valid digits in the string include any digit (`0`-`9`), '`A`', '`B`', '`C`', '`D`', '`#`', and '`*`'. You can also use '`w`' to insert a half-second pause and '`W`' to insert a one-second pause. For example, to pause for one second after connecting and then dial extension 1234 followed by the # key, set this parameter to `W1234#`. Be sure to URL-encode this string because the '`#`' character has special meaning in a URL. If both `SendDigits` and `MachineDetection` parameters are provided, then `MachineDetection` will be ignored.

params.name: SipAuthPassword
params.description: The password required to authenticate the user account specified in `sip_auth_username`.

params.name: SipAuthUsername
params.description: The username used to authenticate the caller making a SIP call.

params.name: StatusCallback
params.description: The URL we should call using the `status_callback_method` to send status information to your application. If no `status_callback_event` is specified, we will send the `completed` status. If an `application_sid` parameter is present, this parameter is ignored. URLs must contain a valid hostname (underscores are not permitted).

params.name: StatusCallbackEvent
params.description: The call progress events that we will send to the `status_callback` URL. Can be: `initiated`, `ringing`, `answered`, and `completed`. If no event is specified, we send the `completed` status. If you want to receive multiple events, specify each one in a separate `status_callback_event` parameter. See the code sample for [monitoring call progress](https://www.twilio.com/docs/voice/api/call-resource?code-sample=code-create-a-call-resource-and-specify-a-statuscallbackevent&code-sdk-version=json). If an `application_sid` is present, this parameter is ignored.

params.name: StatusCallbackMethod
params.description: The HTTP method we should use when calling the `status_callback` URL. Can be: `GET` or `POST` and the default is `POST`. If an `application_sid` parameter is present, this parameter is ignored.

params.name: TimeLimit
params.description: The maximum duration of the call in seconds. Constraints depend on account and configuration.

params.name: Timeout
params.description: The integer number of seconds that we should allow the phone to ring before assuming there is no answer. The default is `60` seconds and the maximum is `600` seconds. For some call flows, we will add a 5-second buffer to the timeout value you provide. For this reason, a timeout value of 10 seconds could result in an actual timeout closer to 15 seconds. You can set this to a short time, such as `15` seconds, to hang up before reaching an answering machine or voicemail.

params.name: To
params.description: The phone number, SIP address, or client identifier to call.

params.name: Trim
params.description: Whether to trim any leading and trailing silence from the recording. Can be: `trim-silence` or `do-not-trim` and the default is `trim-silence`.

params.name: Twiml
params.description: TwiML instructions for the call Twilio will use without fetching Twiml from url parameter. If both `twiml` and `url` are provided then `twiml` parameter will be ignored. Max 4000 characters.

params.name: Url
params.description: The absolute URL that returns the TwiML instructions for the call. We will call this URL using the `method` when the call connects. For more information, see the [Url Parameter](https://www.twilio.com/docs/voice/make-calls#specify-a-url-parameter) section in [Making Calls](https://www.twilio.com/docs/voice/make-calls).

tools.name: calls-json_list-call
tools.description: Retrieves a collection of calls made to and from your account. Required: AccountSid. Optional: To, From, ParentCallSid (plus 10 more). Returns the ListCallResponse.

params.name: AccountSid
params.description: The SID of the [Account](https://www.twilio.com/docs/iam/api/account) that created the Call resource(s) to read.

params.name: To
params.description: Only show calls made to this phone number, SIP address, Client identifier or SIM SID.

params.name: From
params.description: Only include calls from this phone number, SIP address, Client identifier or SIM SID.

params.name: ParentCallSid
params.description: Only include calls spawned by calls with this SID.

params.name: Status
params.description: The status of the calls to include. Can be: `queued`, `ringing`, `in-progress`, `canceled`, `completed`, `failed`, `busy`, or `no-answer`.

params.name: StartTime
params.description: Only include calls that started on this date. Specify a date as `YYYY-MM-DD` in UTC, for example: `2009-07-06`, to read only calls that started on this date.

params.name: StartTime<
params.description: Only include calls that started before this date. Specify a date as `YYYY-MM-DD` in UTC, for example: `2009-07-06`, to read only calls that started before this date.

params.name: StartTime>
params.description: Only include calls that started on or after this date. Specify a date as `YYYY-MM-DD` in UTC, for example: `2009-07-06`, to read only calls that started on or after this date.

params.name: EndTime
params.description: Only include calls that ended on this date. Specify a date as `YYYY-MM-DD` in UTC, for example: `2009-07-06`, to read only calls that ended on this date.

params.name: EndTime<
params.description: Only include calls that ended before this date. Specify a date as `YYYY-MM-DD` in UTC, for example: `2009-07-06`, to read only calls that ended before this date.

params.name: EndTime>
params.description: Only include calls that ended on or after this date. Specify a date as `YYYY-MM-DD` in UTC, for example: `2009-07-06`, to read only calls that ended on or after this date.

params.name: PageSize
params.description: How many resources to return in each list page. The default is 50, and the maximum is 1000.

params.name: Page
params.description: The page index. This value is simply for client state.

params.name: PageToken
params.description: The page token. This is provided by the API.

tools.description: Update the properties of the participant. Required: AccountSid, ConferenceSid, CallSid. Optional: AnnounceMethod, AnnounceUrl, BeepOnExit (plus 9 more). Returns the new ApiV2010AccountConferenceParticipant.

params.name: AccountSid

params.name: ConferenceSid
params.description: The SID of the conference with the participant to update.

params.name: CallSid

params.name: AnnounceMethod
params.description: The HTTP method we should use to call `announce_url`. Can be: `GET` or `POST` and defaults to `POST`.

params.name: AnnounceUrl
params.description: The URL we call using the `announce_method` for an announcement to the participant. The URL may return an MP3 file, a WAV file, or a TwiML document that contains `<Play>`, `<Say>`, `<Pause>`, or `<Redirect>` verbs.

params.name: BeepOnExit
params.description: Whether to play a notification beep to the conference when the participant exits. Can be: `true` or `false`.

params.name: CallSidToCoach
params.description: The SID of the participant who is being `coached`. The participant being coached is the only participant who can hear the participant who is `coaching`.

params.name: Coaching
params.description: Whether the participant is coaching another call. Can be: `true` or `false`. If not present, defaults to `false` unless `call_sid_to_coach` is defined. If `true`, `call_sid_to_coach` must be defined.

params.name: EndConferenceOnExit
params.description: Whether to end the conference when the participant leaves. Can be: `true` or `false` and defaults to `false`.

params.name: Hold
params.description: Whether the participant should be on hold. Can be: `true` or `false`. `true` puts the participant on hold, and `false` lets them rejoin the conference.

params.name: HoldMethod
params.description: The HTTP method we should use to call `hold_url`. Can be: `GET` or `POST` and the default is `GET`.

params.name: HoldUrl
params.description: The URL we call using the `hold_method` for music that plays when the participant is on hold. The URL may return an MP3 file, a WAV file, or a TwiML document that contains `<Play>`, `<Say>`, `<Pause>`, or `<Redirect>` verbs.

params.name: Muted
params.description: Whether the participant should be muted. Can be `true` or `false`. `true` will mute the participant, and `false` will un-mute them. Anything value other than `true` or `false` is interpreted as `false`.

params.name: WaitMethod
params.description: The HTTP method we should use to call `wait_url`. Can be `GET` or `POST` and the default is `POST`. When using a static audio file, this should be `GET` so that we can cache the file.

params.name: WaitUrl
params.description: The URL that Twilio calls using the `wait_method` before the conference has started. The URL may return an MP3 file, a WAV file, or a TwiML document. The default value is the URL of our standard hold music. If you do not want anything to play while waiting for the conference to start, specify an empty string by setting `wait_url` to `''`. For more details on the allowable verbs within the `waitUrl`, see the `waitUrl` attribute in the [<Conference> TwiML instruction](https://www.twilio.com/docs/voice/twiml/conference#attributes-waiturl).

tools.name: conferences_participants-json_create-participant
tools.description: Create participant. Required: AccountSid, ConferenceSid, From, To. Optional: AmdStatusCallback, AmdStatusCallbackMethod, Beep (plus 46 more). Returns the new ApiV2010AccountConferenceParticipant.

params.name: AccountSid
params.description: The SID of the [Account](https://www.

params.name: ConferenceSid
params.description: The SID of the participant's conference.

params.name: AmdStatusCallback
params.description: The URL that we should call using the `amd_status_callback_method` to notify customer application whether the call was answered by human, machine or fax.

params.name: AmdStatusCallbackMethod
params.description: The HTTP method we should use when calling the `amd_status_callback` URL. Can be: `GET` or `POST` and the default is `POST`.

params.name: Beep
params.description: Whether to play a notification beep to the conference when the participant joins. Can be: `true`, `false`, `onEnter`, or `onExit`. The default value is `true`.

params.name: Byoc
params.description: The SID of a BYOC (Bring Your Own Carrier) trunk to route this call with. Note that `byoc` is only meaningful when `to` is a phone number; it will otherwise be ignored. (Beta)

params.name: CallReason
params.description: The Reason for the outgoing call. Use it to specify the purpose of the call that is presented on the called party's phone. (Branded Calls Beta)

params.name: CallSidToCoach
params.description: The SID of the participant who is being `coached`. The participant being coached is the only participant who can hear the participant who is `coaching`.

params.name: CallToken
params.description: A token string needed to invoke a forwarded call. A call_token is generated when an incoming call is received on a Twilio number. Pass an incoming call's call_token value to a forwarded call via the call_token parameter when creating a new call. A forwarded call should bear the same CallerID of the original incoming call.

params.name: CallerDisplayName
params.description: The name that populates the display name in the From header. Must be between 2 and 255 characters. Only applicable for calls to sip address.

params.name: CallerId
params.description: The phone number, Client identifier, or username portion of SIP address that made this call. Phone numbers are in [E.164](https://www.twilio.com/docs/glossary/what-e164) format (e.g., +16175551212). Client identifiers are formatted `client:name`. If using a phone number, it must be a Twilio number or a Verified [outgoing caller id](https://www.twilio.com/docs/voice/api/outgoing-caller-ids) for your account. If the `to` parameter is a phone number, `callerId` must also be a phone number. If `to` is sip address, this value of `callerId` should be a username portion to be used to populate the From header that is passed to the SIP endpoint.

params.name: ClientNotificationUrl
params.description: The URL that we should use to deliver `push call notification`.

params.name: Coaching
params.description: Whether the participant is coaching another call. Can be: `true` or `false`. If not present, defaults to `false` unless `call_sid_to_coach` is defined. If `true`, `call_sid_to_coach` must be defined.

params.name: ConferenceRecord
params.description: Whether to record the conference the participant is joining. Can be: `true`, `false`, `record-from-start`, and `do-not-record`. The default value is `false`.

params.name: ConferenceRecordingStatusCallback
params.description: The URL we should call using the `conference_recording_status_callback_method` when the conference recording is available.

params.name: ConferenceRecordingStatusCallbackEvent
params.description: The conference recording state changes that generate a call to `conference_recording_status_callback`. Can be: `in-progress`, `completed`, `failed`, and `absent`. Separate multiple values with a space, ex: `'in-progress completed failed'`

params.name: ConferenceRecordingStatusCallbackMethod
params.description: The HTTP method we should use to call `conference_recording_status_callback`. Can be: `GET` or `POST` and defaults to `POST`.

params.name: ConferenceStatusCallback
params.description: The URL we should call using the `conference_status_callback_method` when the conference events in `conference_status_callback_event` occur. Only the value set by the first participant to join the conference is used. Subsequent `conference_status_callback` values are ignored.

params.name: ConferenceStatusCallbackEvent
params.description: The conference state changes that should generate a call to `conference_status_callback`. Can be: `start`, `end`, `join`, `leave`, `mute`, `hold`, `modify`, `speaker`, and `announcement`. Separate multiple values with a space. Defaults to `start end`.

params.name: ConferenceStatusCallbackMethod
params.description: The HTTP method we should use to call `conference_status_callback`. Can be: `GET` or `POST` and defaults to `POST`.

params.name: ConferenceTrim
params.description: Whether to trim leading and trailing silence from the conference recording. Can be: `trim-silence` or `do-not-trim` and defaults to `trim-silence`.

params.name: EarlyMedia
params.description: Whether to allow an agent to hear the state of the outbound call, including ringing or disconnect messages. Can be: `true` or `false` and defaults to `true`.

params.name: EndConferenceOnExit
params.description: Whether to end the conference when the participant leaves. Can be: `true` or `false` and defaults to `false`.

params.name: From
params.description: The phone number, Client identifier, or username portion of SIP address that made this call. Phone numbers are in [E.164](https://www.twilio.com/docs/glossary/what-e164) format (e.g., +16175551212). Client identifiers are formatted `client:name`. If using a phone number, it must be a Twilio number or a Verified [outgoing caller id](https://www.twilio.com/docs/voice/api/outgoing-caller-ids) for your account. If the `to` parameter is a phone number, `from` must also be a phone number. If `to` is sip address, this value of `from` should be a username portion to be used to populate the P-Asserted-Identity header that is passed to the SIP endpoint.

params.name: JitterBufferSize
params.description: Jitter buffer size for the connecting participant. Twilio will use this setting to apply Jitter Buffer before participant's audio is mixed into the conference. Can be: `off`, `small`, `medium`, and `large`. Default to `large`.

params.name: Label
params.description: A label for this participant. If one is supplied, it may subsequently be used to fetch, update or delete the participant.

params.name: MachineDetection
params.description: Whether to detect if a human, answering machine, or fax has picked up the call. Can be: `Enable` or `DetectMessageEnd`. Use `Enable` if you would like us to return `AnsweredBy` as soon as the called party is identified. Use `DetectMessageEnd`, if you would like to leave a message on an answering machine. For more information, see [Answering Machine Detection](https://www.twilio.com/docs/voice/answering-machine-detection).

params.name: MachineDetectionSilenceTimeout
params.description: The number of milliseconds of initial silence after which an `unknown` AnsweredBy result will be returned. Possible Values: 2000-10000. Default: 5000.

params.name: MachineDetectionSpeechEndThreshold
params.description: The number of milliseconds of silence after speech activity at which point the speech activity is considered complete. Possible Values: 500-5000. Default: 1200.

params.name: MachineDetectionSpeechThreshold
params.description: The number of milliseconds that is used as the measuring stick for the length of the speech activity, where durations lower than this value will be interpreted as a human and longer than this value as a machine. Possible Values: 1000-6000. Default: 2400.

params.name: MachineDetectionTimeout
params.description: The number of seconds that we should attempt to detect an answering machine before timing out and sending a voice request with `AnsweredBy` of `unknown`. The default timeout is 30 seconds.

params.name: MaxParticipants
params.description: The maximum number of participants in the conference. Can be a positive integer from `2` to `250`. The default value is `250`.

params.name: Muted
params.description: Whether the agent is muted in the conference. Can be `true` or `false` and the default is `false`.

params.name: Record
params.description: Whether to record the participant and their conferences, including the time between conferences. Can be `true` or `false` and the default is `false`.

params.name: RecordingChannels
params.description: The recording channels for the final recording. Can be: `mono` or `dual` and the default is `mono`.

params.name: RecordingConfigurationId
params.description: The identifier of the configuration to be used when creating and processing the recording

params.name: RecordingStatusCallback
params.description: The URL that we should call using the `recording_status_callback_method` when the recording status changes.

params.name: RecordingStatusCallbackEvent
params.description: The recording state changes that should generate a call to `recording_status_callback`. Can be: `started`, `in-progress`, `paused`, `resumed`, `stopped`, `completed`, `failed`, and `absent`. Separate multiple values with a space, ex: `'in-progress completed failed'`.

params.name: RecordingStatusCallbackMethod
params.description: The HTTP method we should use when we call `recording_status_callback`. Can be: `GET` or `POST` and defaults to `POST`.

params.name: RecordingTrack
params.description: The audio track to record for the call. Can be: `inbound`, `outbound` or `both`. The default is `both`. `inbound` records the audio that is received by Twilio. `outbound` records the audio that is sent from Twilio. `both` records the audio that is received and sent by Twilio.

params.name: Region
params.description: The [region](https://support.twilio.com/hc/en-us/articles/223132167-How-global-low-latency-routing-and-region-selection-work-for-conferences-and-Client-calls) where we should mix the recorded audio. Can be:`us1`, `us2`, `ie1`, `de1`, `sg1`, `br1`, `au1`, or `jp1`.

params.name: SipAuthPassword
params.description: The SIP password for authentication.

params.name: SipAuthUsername
params.description: The SIP username used for authentication.

params.name: StartConferenceOnEnter
params.description: Whether to start the conference when the participant joins, if it has not already started. Can be: `true` or `false` and the default is `true`. If `false` and the conference has not started, the participant is muted and hears background music until another participant starts the conference.

params.name: StatusCallback
params.description: The URL we should call using the `status_callback_method` to send status information to your...

params.name: StatusCallbackEvent
params.description: The conference state changes that should generate a call to `status_callback`. Can be: `initiated`, `ringing`, `answered`, and `completed`. Separate multiple values with a space. The default value is `completed`.

params.name: StatusCallbackMethod
params.description: The HTTP method we should use to call `status_callback`. Can be: `GET` and `POST` and defaults to `POST`.

params.name: TimeLimit
params.description: The maximum duration of the call in seconds. Constraints depend on account and configuration.

params.name: Timeout
params.description: The number of seconds that we should allow the phone to ring before assuming there is no answer. Can be an integer between `5` and `600`, inclusive. The default value is `60`. We always add a 5-second timeout buffer to outgoing calls, so value of 10 would result in an actual timeout that was closer to 15 seconds.

params.name: To
params.description: The phone number, SIP address, Client, TwiML App identifier that received this call. Phone numbers are in [E.164](https://www.twilio.com/docs/glossary/what-e164) format (e.g., +16175551212). SIP addresses are formatted as `sip:name@company.com`. Client identifiers are formatted `client:name`. TwiML App identifiers are formatted `app:<APP_SID>`. [Custom parameters](https://www.twilio.com/docs/voice/api/conference-participant-resource#custom-parameters) may also be specified.

params.name: Trim
params.description: Whether to trim any leading and trailing silence from the participant recording. Can be: `trim-silence` or `do-not-trim` and the default is `trim-silence`.

params.name: WaitMethod
params.description: The HTTP method we should use to call `wait_url`. Can be `GET` or `POST` and the default is `POST`. When using a static audio file, this should be `GET` so that we can cache the file.

params.name: WaitUrl
params.description: The URL that Twilio calls using the `wait_method` before the conference has started. The URL may return an MP3 file, a WAV file, or a TwiML document. The default value is the URL of our standard hold music. If you do not want anything to play while waiting for the conference to start, specify an empty string by setting `wait_url` to `''`. For more details on the allowable verbs within the `waitUrl`, see the `waitUrl` attribute in the [<Conference> TwiML instruction](https://www.twilio.com/docs/voice/twiml/conference#attributes-waiturl).

tools.name: messages-json_create-message
tools.description: Send a message. Required: AccountSid, To. Optional: AddressRetention, ApplicationSid, Attempt (plus 21 more). Returns the new ApiV2010AccountMessage.

params.name: AccountSid
params.description: The SID of the [Account](https://www.twilio.com/docs/iam/api/account) creating the Message resource.

params.name: AddressRetention
params.description: Determines if the address can be stored or obfuscated based on privacy settings

params.name: ApplicationSid
params.description: The SID of the associated [TwiML Application](https://www.twilio.com/docs/usage/api/applications). [Message status callback requests](https://www.twilio.com/docs/sms/api/message-resource#twilios-request-to-the-statuscallback-url) are sent to the TwiML App's `message_status_callback` URL. Note that the `status_callback` parameter of a request takes priority over the `application_sid` parameter; if both are included `application_sid` is ignored.

params.name: Attempt
params.description: Total number of attempts made (including this request) to send the message regardless of the provider used

params.name: Body
params.description: The text content of the outgoing message. Can be up to 1,600 characters in length. SMS only: If the `body` contains more than 160 [GSM-7](https://www.twilio.com/docs/glossary/what-is-gsm-7-character-encoding) characters (or 70 [UCS-2](https://www.twilio.com/docs/glossary/what-is-ucs-2-character-encoding) characters), the message is segmented and charged accordingly. For long `body` text, consider using the [send_as_mms parameter](https://www.twilio.com/blog/mms-for-long-text-messages).

params.name: ContentRetention
params.description: Determines if the message content can be stored or redacted based on privacy settings

params.name: ContentSid
params.description: For [Content Editor/API](https://www.twilio.com/docs/content) only: The SID of the Content Template to be used with the Message, e.g., `HXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX`. If this parameter is not provided, a Content Template is not used. Find the SID in the Console on the Content Editor page. For Content API users, the SID is found in Twilio's response when [creating the Template](https://www.twilio.com/docs/content/content-api-resources#create-templates) or by [fetching your Templates](https://www.twilio.com/docs/content/content-api-resources#fetch-all-content-resources).

params.name: ContentVariables
params.description: For [Content Editor/API](https://www.twilio.com/docs/content) only: Key-value pairs of [Template variables](https://www.twilio.com/docs/content/using-variables-with-content-api) and their substitution values. `content_sid` parameter must also be provided. If values are not defined in the `content_variables` parameter, the [Template's default placeholder values](https://www.twilio.com/docs/content/content-api-resources#create-templates) are used.

params.name: FallbackFrom
params.description: A fallback SMS sender to use when the recipient cannot be reached over RCS. This parameter may only be used when also providing a [Messaging Service](https://twilio.com/docs/messaging/services) containing an RCS sender. The fallback SMS sender must be either a Twilio phone number (in [E.164](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/E.164) format), [alphanumeric sender ID](https://www.twilio.com/docs/sms/quickstart), or [short code](https://www.twilio.com/en-us/messaging/channels/sms/short-codes), hosted within Twilio and belong to the Account creating the Message.

params.name: ForceDelivery
params.description: Reserved

params.name: From
params.description: The sender's Twilio phone number (in [E.164](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/E.164) format), [alphanumeric sender ID](https://www.twilio.com/docs/sms/quickstart), [Wireless SIM](https://www.twilio.com/docs/iot/wireless/programmable-wireless-send-machine-machine-sms-commands), [short code](https://www.twilio.com/en-us/messaging/channels/sms/short-codes), or [channel address](https://www.twilio.com/docs/messaging/channels) (e.g., `whatsapp:+15554449999`). The value of the `from` parameter must be a sender that is hosted within Twilio and belongs to the Account creating the Message. If you are using `messaging_service_sid`, this parameter can be empty (Twilio assigns a `from` value from the Messaging Service's Sender Pool) or you can provide a specific sender from your Sender Pool.

params.name: MaxPrice
params.description: [OBSOLETE] This parameter will no longer have any effect as of 2024-06-03.

params.name: MediaUrl
params.description: The URL of media to include in the Message content. `jpeg`, `jpg`, `gif`, and `png` file types are fully supported by Twilio and content is formatted for delivery on destination devices. The media size limit is 5 MB for supported file types (`jpeg`, `jpg`, `png`, `gif`) and 500 KB for [other types](https://www.twilio.com/docs/messaging/guides/accepted-mime-types) of accepted media. To send more than one image in the message, provide multiple `media_url` parameters in the POST request. You can include up to ten `media_url` parameters per message. [International](https://support.twilio.com/hc/en-us/articles/223179808-Sending-and-receiving-MMS-messages) and [carrier](https://support.twilio.com/hc/en-us/articles/223133707-Is-MMS-supported-for-all-carriers-in-US-and-Canada-) limits apply.

params.name: MessagingServiceSid
params.description: The SID of the [Messaging Service](https://www.twilio.com/docs/messaging/services) you want to associate with the Message. When this parameter is provided and the `from` parameter is omitted, Twilio selects the optimal sender from the Messaging Service's Sender Pool. You may also provide a `from` parameter if you want to use a specific Sender from the Sender Pool.

params.name: PersistentAction
params.description: Rich actions for non-SMS/MMS channels. Used for [sending location in WhatsApp messages](https://www.twilio.com/docs/whatsapp/message-features#location-messages-with-whatsapp).

params.name: ProvideFeedback
params.description: Boolean indicating whether or not you intend to provide delivery confirmation feedback to Twilio (used in conjunction with the [Message Feedback subresource](https://www.twilio.com/docs/sms/api/message-feedback-resource)). Default value is `false`.

params.name: RiskCheck
params.description: Include this parameter with a value of `disable` to skip any kind of risk check on the respective message request.

params.name: ScheduleType
params.description: For Messaging Services only: Include this parameter with a value of `fixed` in conjuction with the `send_time` parameter in order to [schedule a Message](https://www.twilio.com/docs/messaging/features/message-scheduling).

params.name: SendAsMms
params.description: If set to `true`, Twilio delivers the message as a single MMS message, regardless of the presence of media.

params.name: SendAt
params.description: The time that Twilio will send the message. Must be in ISO 8601 format.

params.name: ShortenUrls
params.description: For Messaging Services with [Link Shortening configured](https://www.twilio.com/docs/messaging/features/link-shortening) only: A Boolean indicating whether or not Twilio should shorten links in the `body` of the Message. Default value is `false`. If `true`, the `messaging_service_sid` parameter must also be provided.

params.name: SmartEncoded
params.description: Whether to detect Unicode characters that have a similar GSM-7 character and replace them. Can be: `true` or `false`.

params.name: StatusCallback
params.description: The URL of the endpoint to which Twilio sends [Message status callback requests](https://www.twilio.com/docs/sms/api/message-resource#twilios-request-to-the-statuscallback-url). URL must contain a valid hostname and underscores are not allowed. If you include this parameter with the `messaging_service_sid`, Twilio uses this URL instead of the Status Callback URL of the [Messaging Service](https://www.twilio.com/docs/messaging/api/service-resource).

params.name: To
params.description: The recipient's phone number in [E.164](https://www.twilio.com/docs/glossary/what-e164) format (for SMS/MMS) or [channel address](https://www.twilio.com/docs/messaging/channels), e.g. `whatsapp:+15552229999`.

params.name: TrafficType
params.description: Traffic type

params.name: ValidityPeriod
params.description: The maximum length in seconds that the Message can remain in Twilio's outgoing message queue. If a queued Message exceeds the `validity_period`, the Message is not sent. Accepted values are integers from `1` to `36000`. Default value is `36000`. A `validity_period` greater than `5` is recommended. [Learn more about the validity period](https://www.twilio.com/blog/take-more-control-of-outbound-messages-using-validity-period-html)

tools.name: sip_create-domain
tools.description: Create a new Domain. Required: AccountSid, DomainName. Optional: ByocTrunkSid, EmergencyCallerSid, EmergencyCallingEnabled (plus 9 more). Returns the new ApiV2010AccountSipSipDomain.

params.name: AccountSid
params.description: The SID of the [Account](https://www.

params.name: ByocTrunkSid
params.description: The SID of the BYOC Trunk(Bring Your Own Carrier) resource that the Sip Domain will be associated with.

params.name: DomainName
params.description: The unique address you reserve on Twilio to which you route your SIP traffic. Domain names can contain letters, digits, and '-' and must end with `sip.twilio.com`.

params.name: EmergencyCallerSid
params.description: Whether an emergency caller sid is configured for the domain. If present, this phone number will be used as the callback for the emergency call.

params.name: EmergencyCallingEnabled
params.description: Whether emergency calling is enabled for the domain. If enabled, allows emergency calls on the domain from phone numbers with validated addresses.

params.name: FriendlyName
params.description: A descriptive string that you created to describe the resource. It can be up to 64 characters long.

params.name: Secure
params.description: Whether secure SIP is enabled for the domain. If enabled, TLS will be enforced and SRTP will be negotiated on all incoming calls to this sip domain.

params.name: SipRegistration
params.description: Whether to allow SIP Endpoints to register with the domain to receive calls. Can be `true` or `false`. `true` allows SIP Endpoints to register with the domain to receive calls, `false` does not.

params.name: VoiceFallbackMethod
params.description: The HTTP method we should use to call `voice_fallback_url`. Can be: `GET` or `POST`.

params.name: VoiceFallbackUrl
params.description: The URL that we should call when an error occurs while retrieving or executing the TwiML from `voice_url`.

params.name: VoiceMethod
params.description: The HTTP method we should use to call `voice_url`. Can be: `GET` or `POST`.

params.name: VoiceStatusCallbackMethod
params.description: The HTTP method we should use to call `voice_status_callback_url`. Can be: `GET` or `POST`.

params.name: VoiceStatusCallbackUrl
params.description: The URL that we should call to pass status parameters (such as call ended) to your application.

params.name: VoiceUrl
params.description: The URL we should when the domain receives a call.

params.name: FriendlyName

params.name: Secure
params.description: Whether secure SIP is enabled for the domain. If enabled, TLS will be enforced and SRTP will be negotiated on all incoming calls to this sip domain.

params.name: SipRegistration

params.name: VoiceStatusCallbackUrl
